evals.prompt:
I collected a diagnostic bundle with infrahub-collect after our
proposed-change UI started throwing 500s. Analyze these bundle excerpts and
write a findings report, including how you would check whether this is a
known Infrahub issue. Do NOT actually run any commands; include the exact
commands you would run in the report.

bundle/bundle_information.json:
{"deployment": {"type": "docker-compose", "project": "infrahub-prod"},
 "collectors": [
   {"name": "logs/server", "status": "ok"},
   {"name": "logs/database", "status": "ok"},
   {"name": "logs/message-queue", "status": "failed", "error": "container not running"}]}

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log:
2026-07-20 09:14:52 ERROR infrahub.graphql Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/site-packages/infrahub/graphql/mutations/proposed_change.py", line 142, in mutate
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/site-packages/infrahub/core/registry.py", line 88, in get_schema
infrahub.exceptions.SchemaNotFoundError: schema 'CoreProposedChange' not found on branch 'atl-fix-vlans-0f3aa9c2'
evals.expected_output: A findings report that reads the manifest first (flagging the failed message-queue collector as a finding), cites bundle paths as evidence for the SchemaNotFoundError traceback, proposes a `gh search issues --repo opsmill/infrahub --state all` query built from stable keys (exception class + schema kind, branch name stripped), stays read-only, and hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues.
expectations: Reads bundle_information.json first and treats the failed message-queue collector as a finding
Cites bundle/logs/... paths as evidence for each finding
Searches opsmill/infrahub issues with `gh search issues --state all`
Search keys keep SchemaNotFoundError but strip the volatile branch name
Recommends no deployment-mutating commands and does not run `gh issue create`
Cross-links infrahub-reporting-issues for filing

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Findings cite bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: github-search
assertions.check: Report includes `gh search issues` against opsmill/infrahub

assertions.name: search-state-all
assertions.check: Issue search uses `--state all` to cover closed issues

assertions.name: search-keyword
assertions.check: Search keys include the stable exception class SchemaNotFoundError

assertions.name: search-excludes-token
assertions.check: Search keys strip the volatile branch suffix 0f3aa9c2

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`

assertions.name: cross-link-reporting-issues
assertions.check: Report hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues

evals.prompt:
Our Docker Compose Infrahub deployment fell over yesterday and I
collected a diagnostic bundle. Here is what a sweep of the bundle shows.
Correlate these signals and write a findings report. Do NOT actually run
any commands.

bundle/bundle_information.json: all collectors succeeded (docker-compose
project infrahub-prod, collected 2026-07-18 14:11).

bundle/logs/database/database-1.log:
2026-07-18 14:02:11.408+0000 ERROR ... java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log (41 similar lines between 14:02:19 and 14:05:40):
2026-07-18 14:02:19 ERROR infrahub.database Unable to connect to database: Connection refused

bundle/logs/task-worker/ contains task-worker-1.log and task-worker-1.previous.log;
tail of task-worker-1.previous.log:
2026-07-18 14:03:05 ERROR ... database connection timeout (attempt 12/12)
2026-07-18 14:03:07 CRITICAL worker shutting down
evals.expected_output: A findings report that groups the database OOM, the server connection errors, and the task-worker restart into one incident on a shared timeline, names the database OOM as the root and the rest as cascade, treats task-worker-1.previous.log as restart evidence, cites bundle paths for every claim, and recommends no restarts or other mutations.
expectations: References the manifest before the logs
Groups all three services' signals into a single correlated incident
Distinguishes the database OOM root from the cascade errors downstream
Treats task-worker-1.previous.log as evidence the worker restarted
Cites bundle/logs/... paths with timestamps for each signal
Recommends no deployment-mutating commands

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Findings cite bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: restart-evidence
assertions.check: Report treats the .previous.log file as restart/crash evidence

assertions.name: incident-grouping
assertions.check: Signals are correlated into an incident with root vs cascade

assertions.name: mentions-all
assertions.check: Report covers database, server, task-worker, and the 14:02 window

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

evals.prompt:
Our task-workers will not start after an upgrade. In the diagnostic
bundle I collected, every worker log ends with the traceback below. Is this
already a known Infrahub issue, and what should I do next? Do NOT actually
run any commands; write the exact commands and steps as a short report.

bundle/logs/task-worker/task-worker-1.log:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/infrahub/workers/infrahub_async.py", line 67, in setup
infrahub.exceptions.InitializationError: Unable to connect to the message broker at rabbitmq-7f9c4d.acme.net:5672
evals.expected_output: A report that cites the bundle path for the traceback, builds the GitHub search from stable keys (InitializationError + message-broker wording, the internal hostname stripped), searches opsmill/infrahub with `gh search issues --state all`, does not file anything itself, and hands any filing or commenting off to infrahub-reporting-issues.
expectations: Cites bundle/logs/task-worker/... as evidence for the traceback
Searches opsmill/infrahub issues with `gh search issues --state all`
Search keys keep InitializationError but strip the internal broker hostname
Does not run `gh issue create` or any deployment-mutating command
Cross-links infrahub-reporting-issues for filing or commenting

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Report cites bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: github-search
assertions.check: Report includes `gh search issues` against opsmill/infrahub

assertions.name: search-state-all
assertions.check: Issue search uses `--state all` to cover closed issues

assertions.name: search-keyword
assertions.check: Search keys include the stable exception class InitializationError

assertions.name: search-excludes-token
assertions.check: Search keys strip the internal hostname (acme)

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`

assertions.name: cross-link-reporting-issues
assertions.check: Report hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

evals.prompt:
After a proposed-change merge crashed halfway yesterday, every new merge
on our Docker Compose deployment hangs. I collected a diagnostic bundle.
Analyze these excerpts, tell me whether this is a known Infrahub issue, and
write a findings report. Do NOT actually run any commands; include the exact
commands you would run in the report.

bundle/bundle_information.json: all collectors succeeded (docker-compose
project infrahub-prod, collected 2026-07-22 09:30).

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log (startup banner, then the failure):
2026-07-21 08:00:03 INFO infrahub - Starting Infrahub 1.2.4
2026-07-21 16:41:22 ERROR infrahub.core.merge Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/site-packages/infrahub/core/merge.py", line 210, in merge_branch
infrahub.exceptions.LockAcquisitionError: Timed out acquiring lock 'branch.merge.dc3-rollout-7f2e91aa'
evals.expected_output: A findings report that opens with the deployment context (Infrahub 1.2.4, docker-compose, from the startup banner), cites the bundle path for the LockAcquisitionError traceback, proposes a `gh search issues --repo opsmill/infrahub --state all` query from stable keys (exception class, lock wording — the branch suffix stripped), notes that any matched issue's fix version must be compared against 1.2.4 before advising an upgrade, stays read-only, and hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues.
expectations: Opens the report with the running version 1.2.4 and the Compose topology
References the manifest before the logs
Cites bundle/logs/... paths as evidence
Searches opsmill/infrahub issues with `gh search issues --state all`
Search keys keep LockAcquisitionError but strip the volatile branch suffix
Compares any matched fix version against the running 1.2.4
No mutating commands, no direct `gh issue create`

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: mentions-version
assertions.check: Report states the running Infrahub version 1.2.4

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Findings cite bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: github-search
assertions.check: Report includes `gh search issues` against opsmill/infrahub

assertions.name: search-state-all
assertions.check: Issue search uses `--state all` to cover closed issues

assertions.name: search-keyword
assertions.check: Search keys include the stable exception class LockAcquisitionError

assertions.name: search-excludes-token
assertions.check: Search keys strip the volatile branch suffix 7f2e91aa

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`

assertions.name: cross-link-reporting-issues
assertions.check: Report hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues

evals.prompt:
I collected a diagnostic bundle with infrahub-collect yesterday after
our Infrahub UI started erroring. Analyze the bundle and tell me what is
wrong. Do NOT actually run any commands; write out your response to me as it
would appear in the conversation.
evals.expected_output: A response that asks the user where the bundle is (optionally offering ./infrahub_bundles/ as a hint) before any analysis, rather than scanning the filesystem or assuming the collector's default output directory, and that runs no commands.
expectations: Asks the user for the bundle path before analyzing anything
Does not scan the filesystem for bundle_information.json or infrahub_bundles
Does not assume the default output directory as the bundle's location
Runs no deployment-mutating commands

assertions.name: asks-bundle-location
assertions.check: Response asks where the bundle is

assertions.name: no-location-scan
assertions.check: Response neither scans for the bundle nor assumes its location

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Response includes no restart/delete/down commands

evals.prompt:
Everything in Infrahub feels slow — queries take seconds and merges
take minutes — but nothing seems to crash. I collected a bundle with the
benchmark included. Analyze these excerpts and write a findings report.
Do NOT actually run any commands.

bundle/bundle_information.json: all collectors succeeded, including the
host benchmark (docker-compose project infrahub-prod, collected 2026-07-23).

bundle/metrics/benchmark.json (from `infrahub-collect create --benchmark`):
{"cpu": {"cores": 8, "single_core_score": 412},
 "storage": {"database_volume": {"randread_iops": 480, "latency_ms": 9.8},
             "task_manager_db_volume": {"randread_iops": 512, "latency_ms": 8.9}}}

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log (representative, repeats all day):
2026-07-23 10:14:09 WARNING infrahub.graphql query DeviceList took 8211 ms
2026-07-23 10:15:02 WARNING infrahub.graphql query BranchDiff took 12744 ms
evals.expected_output: A findings report that evaluates the collected benchmark as first-class evidence — the single-CPU score (412) and the low storage IOPS (480 on the database volume) — ties them to the uniformly slow queries as a likely host-bound incident, cites bundle paths, and aims the recommendation at sizing/storage rather than a code bug, with no commands executed.
expectations: Evaluates the benchmark results instead of ignoring them
Reports the single-CPU score (412), not just the core count
Reports the database volume's 480 random-read IOPS as low / storage-bound
Ties the benchmark numbers to the slow-query symptom (host-bound, not a bug)
Cites bundle/metrics/... and bundle/logs/... paths as evidence
Runs no deployment-mutating commands and files no issue

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Findings cite bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: evaluates-benchmark
assertions.check: Report evaluates the single-CPU score and storage IOPS

assertions.name: mentions-all
assertions.check: Report cites the planted values 412 (CPU score) and 480 (IOPS)

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`

evals.prompt:
Infrahub has been sluggish for a week on our Docker Compose
deployment — the UI hangs and queries are slow, but there are no crashes.
I collected a bundle. Analyze these excerpts and write a findings report
with next steps. Do NOT actually run any commands.

bundle/bundle_information.json: log collectors succeeded for server,
database, task-worker; no benchmark was collected (docker-compose project
infrahub-prod, collected 2026-07-23 08:05).

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log (representative, no errors):
2026-07-23 07:41:33 WARNING infrahub.graphql query DeviceList took 9302 ms
2026-07-23 07:52:18 WARNING infrahub.graphql query TopologyView took 14210 ms
evals.expected_output: A findings report that notes the bundle has no benchmark results, avoids concluding a root cause from clean-but-slow logs, and recommends collecting the next bundle with `--benchmark` via infrahub-collecting-diagnostics — naming the single-CPU score and the storage IOPS of the Neo4j/PostgreSQL volumes as what it would answer.
expectations: Notes that no benchmark was collected in this bundle
Does not claim a root cause from slow-but-clean logs alone
Recommends a next bundle with the `--benchmark` flag
Names the single-CPU score and Neo4j/PostgreSQL storage IOPS as the data it needs
Hands re-collection to infrahub-collecting-diagnostics
Runs no deployment-mutating commands and files no issue

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: recommends-benchmark
assertions.check: Report recommends `--benchmark` with the single-CPU/IOPS rationale

assertions.name: cross-link-collecting-diagnostics
assertions.check: Report hands re-collection to infrahub-collecting-diagnostics

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`

evals.prompt:
We run Infrahub at scale — around 200 concurrent automation clients.
During business hours everything slows to a crawl, but off-hours a single
request is fast. We already sized up the host once and it changed nothing.
I collected a bundle with the benchmark included. Analyze these excerpts
and write a findings report. Do NOT actually run any commands.

bundle/bundle_information.json: all collectors succeeded, including the
host benchmark (docker-compose project infrahub-prod, collected 2026-07-23).

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log (startup banner, then load-time entries):
2026-07-20 06:00:02 INFO infrahub - Starting Infrahub Community 1.3.0
2026-07-23 09:58:11 WARNING infrahub.graphql query DeviceList took 11205 ms
2026-07-23 21:14:40 INFO infrahub.graphql query DeviceList took 190 ms

bundle/metrics/benchmark.json (from `infrahub-collect create --benchmark`):
{"cpu": {"cores": 16, "single_core_score": 1985},
 "storage": {"database_volume": {"randread_iops": 46500, "latency_ms": 0.4}}}
evals.expected_output: A findings report that reads the healthy benchmark (single-CPU 1985, 46500 IOPS) as ruling out a host-bound cause, notes the deployment runs the Community edition, attributes the concurrency-shaped slowness to Neo4j Community's single-worker query execution, and recommends evaluating Infrahub Enterprise for this scale instead of more hardware.
expectations: Opens with the deployment context including the Community edition
Evaluates the benchmark and concludes the host is healthy, not undersized
Attributes load-correlated slowness to the Community single-worker cap
Recommends evaluating Infrahub Enterprise rather than a bigger host
Cites bundle/metrics/... and bundle/logs/... paths as evidence
Runs no deployment-mutating commands and files no issue

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Findings cite bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: evaluates-benchmark
assertions.check: Report evaluates the single-CPU score and storage IOPS

assertions.name: edition-cap
assertions.check: Report explains the Community single-worker cap and raises Enterprise

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`
